grammar_patterns.pattern: Station called + this is + vessel name
examples: Marina Control, this is sailing yacht Aurora.
Requesting berth.
Requesting fuel berth.

grammar_patterns.pattern: My heading is + three-digit heading + degrees
examples: My heading is zero nine zero degrees.
My speed is five knots.
My ETA is one four zero zero UTC.
I am reducing speed.
I will pass astern of you.
Request traffic information.
Mayday, Mayday, Mayday.
Pan-Pan, Pan-Pan, Pan-Pan.
Securite, Securite, Securite.
I have engine failure.
I am taking on water.
I am not under command.
There is risk of collision.

grammar_patterns.pattern: I am altering course to + direction
examples: I am altering course to starboard.

grammar_patterns.pattern: I am navigating in + visibility condition
examples: I am navigating in restricted visibility.
Man overboard.
Radio check, over.
How do you read me?
I read you loud and clear.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Called station + this is + vessel name
examples: Aurora, this is sailing yacht Orion.
Marina Control, Aurora calling.
Switch to channel one two.
Read back channel one two.
Correction, channel one three.
Question, are you underway?
Answer, affirmative.
Answer, negative.
Request read back.
Say again your position.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Stand by on channel + channel number
examples: Stand by on channel one six.
Do not answer until called.
Resume communication now.
Wait out, I will call you again.
Correction, my ETA is one five zero zero UTC.
Spell your call sign.

grammar_patterns.pattern: I spell + identifier + spelling alphabet sequence
examples: I spell Aurora: Alfa Uniform Romeo Oscar Romeo Alfa.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Marina station + this is + vessel name
examples: Marina Control, this is sailing yacht Orion.
Request berth for tonight.
Request berth for one night.
Stand by outside the marina.
Stand by outside the harbour entrance.
I am arriving from the south.
Request visitor berth.
Request fresh water.
Request shore power.
Request fuel berth on arrival.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Request + service one + and + service two
examples: Request water and shore power.
Proceed to berth Bravo one two.
Approach at slow speed.
Berth port side to.
Berth starboard side to.
Prepare fenders on port side.
Prepare bow and stern lines.
Come alongside slowly.
Request permission to depart.
I am ready to depart.
I am leaving the berth.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Stand by outside + location + for berth assignment
examples: Stand by outside the marina entrance for berth assignment.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Proceed to berth + berth number + side to
examples: Proceed to berth three, port side to.
After taking fuel, proceed to your berth.
Depart when the fairway is clear.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Approach + manner + and keep + mooring item + ready
examples: Approach slowly and keep fenders ready.

grammar_patterns.pattern: My position is + distance + direction + reference
examples: My position is two nautical miles north of the marina.

grammar_patterns.pattern: My position is + direction + reporting point
examples: My position is east of reporting point Alpha.
I am passing reporting point Alpha.
I am abeam reporting point Bravo.
My heading is zero nine zero degrees.
My course is zero nine zero degrees.
Bearing to marina is two seven zero degrees.

grammar_patterns.pattern: My course is + course + speed + speed value
examples: My course is zero nine zero, speed five knots.
My speed is six knots.
Distance is three nautical miles to the marina.

grammar_patterns.pattern: Distance is + decimal distance + unit + reference
examples: Distance is one decimal five nautical miles to the reporting point.
My ETA is one four zero zero UTC.
ETA update: one five zero zero UTC.

grammar_patterns.pattern: ETA at + reporting point + is + time + UTC
examples: ETA at reporting point Alpha is one four three zero UTC.

grammar_patterns.pattern: I will report at + reporting point
examples: I will report at reporting point Alpha.
Read back my position.
